calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-13-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Rowena Navia appearing via zoom for Ludmila Cydzik (petitioner).

The Court noted that Mr. Kronman is not present. Mrs. Navia stated that Mr. Kronman passed away, that they had a good working relationship and that after Christine Goldman received the personal property, there has been no issue since then. Mrs. Cydzik stated that she had never ever in her life experienced such a big challenge, spoke of the time spent and the services that she provided, spoke of the difficulties and challenges she dealt with and indicated that the amount of hours she is requesting does not reflect the actual amount of hours that she worked. Mrs. Navia stated that Mrs. Cydzik is only half of the seller, that the other half is Christine Goldman and that Mrs. Cydzik took on all of the sale of the property. The second and final account and report of executor, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant. The Court informed the parties that it will be reviewing the fee request.

Judge; Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Cristobal; Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Ms. Navia confirms that accounts are fully insured and offers the specific bank accounts wherein the funds have been distributed and points to a declaration filed yesterday which outlines the preceding. The court commends Ms. Navia and her client for their efforts regarding this matter.

The Status of Transferring Funds Into Insured Accounts is taken off calendar.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Transferring Funds Into Fdic Insured Accounts
Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Continued To Mar-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Ludmila Cydzik, Personal Representative.

The court and Ms. Navia discuss whether the multiple deposits are held in FDIC insured accounts. Ms. Navia provides oral testimony regarding the accounts and is unable to confirm the preceding and advises that she will need to discuss the matter with her client. The court reiterates its concern that the accounts are insured. The court queries Ms. Navia regarding the filing of a petition for final distribution. Ms. Navia reports that a petition for final distribution should be filed as soon as the matter regarding insuring the funds has been resolved.

After brief, additional, discussion, the Status of Transferring Funds Into FDIC Insured Accounts is continued to Mar-16-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Apr-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: J. Jeung. Not reported.

After additional discussion, the Status of Transferring Funds Into FDIC accounts is continued to Jan-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Ms. Navia is instructed to file proof of the transfer of funds with the court by Jan-10-2022.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Continued To Dec-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Ludmilla Cydzik (Conservator).

Ms. Navia stated that there were two pieces of real property, that the first property was sold, that the second property is co-owned by a Stepdaughter, that there was an issue of whether or not the Stepdaughter would purchase the property and that they came to an agreement that the property will be sold. Ms. Navia also stated that the property is now on the market and that they are hopeful that the property will sell by the end of the month. The Court informed Ms. Navia that she still needs to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Status on close of estate is continued to December 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Continued To May-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Ludmilla Cydzik (Executor).

Ms. Navia stated that there are two pieces of real property that were owned by the Decedent and another party, that there was some conflict, that they sold the San Francisco property, and that they are in the process of selling the Brisbane property, but there is some conflict with that as well. Ms. Navia also stated that the party wants to do a 1031 exchange, and requested a 30-day continuance stating that she will file a status report. Status on close of estate is continued to May 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Executor.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that she spoke with Ms. Barrese's Paralegal yesterday and that she was told they are looking for several wills. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that she believes there is a Trust for Ms. Barrese, that they did not see a probated estate, that her will was deposited in San Mateo, that Ms. Barrese's Family members and her Paralegal are going through documents, and that she is not aware of or received any objections. The Court stated that there is enough evidence to infer that the will was not destroyed. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will (order signed in open Court). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for April 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
